body.title: Thread context persistence: fix 'I don't recall what we were talking about'
body.result: store_plan + read_active_goal_plan tools live; PROC_ACTIVE_GOAL_CONTEXT_REFRESH seeded (120s schedule); run_coding_sprint prompt updated to call store_plan before starting. commit f9139a91.
body.description: Igor loses thread context across restarts and cold turns. Symptom: 'I don't recall what we were talking about' — he sees the [CODING SPRINT] prompt but can't hold the reasoning chain across turns. Root causes: (1) TWM and ring buffer reset on restart — active thread's reasoning state is in-process only; (2) no context_inject habit to re-load thread history for active ticket on turn start; (3) tier.2 LLM reasons from scratch each turn, generating plausible-sounding text instead of real tool dispatch. Fix direction: (A) persist active thread reasoning state to DB on every significant step; (B) context_inject habit: on new turn about active goal/ticket, retrieve thread history from DB before BG scoring; (C) facia as thread entry-point — hitting the facia node for a ticket should re-contextualize Igor before he responds. This is the blocker for 'Igor as coder' — without thread continuity, he cannot maintain a coherent implementation session.
